question_types.title: Cubic graphs 4A — Sketching with Linear Factors

questions.question: Sketch the following curve and indicate clearly the points of intersection with the axes: y = (x - 3)(x - 2)(x + 1)
questions.explanation: To find x-intercepts, set y=0. y-intercept: y = (-3)(-2)(1) = 6.
options: Intersects x-axis at (3, 0), (2, 0), (-1, 0). Intersects y-axis at (0, 6).

questions.question: Sketch the following curve and indicate clearly the points of intersection with the axes: y = x(x + 1)(x - 1)
questions.explanation: Roots are x=0, x=-1, x=1. Passes through the origin.
options: Intersects x-axis at (0, 0), (-1, 0), (1, 0). Intersects y-axis at (0, 0).

question_types.title: Cubic graphs 4A — Factored Forms and Coefficients

questions.question: Sketch the following curve and indicate clearly the points of intersection with the axes: y = (x - 2)(2x - 1)(2x + 1)
questions.explanation: Roots are x=2, x=0.5, x=-0.5. y-intercept: (-2)(-1)(1) = 2.
options: Intersects x-axis at (2, 0), (0.5, 0), (-0.5, 0). Intersects y-axis at (0, 2).

questions.question: Sketch the following curve and indicate clearly the points of intersection with the axes: y = (x + 1)(x + 2)(x + 3)(x + 4)
options: Intersects x-axis at (-1, 0), (-2, 0), (-3, 0), (-4, 0). Intersects y-axis at (0, 24).

questions.question: Sketch the following curve and indicate clearly the points of intersection with the axes: y = x²(4x + 1)(4x - 1)
questions.explanation: x=0 is a repeated root so the graph touches the axis there. Other roots are ±0.25.
options: Intersects x-axis at (0, 0), (-0.25, 0), (0.25, 0). Touches x-axis at (0, 0) — repeated root.
x-intercepts at (0, 0), (0.5, 0), (−0.5, 0) with no repeated root

questions.question: Sketch y = 4/x and y = 12/x on separate diagrams.
questions.explanation: Both are y=k/x with k>0. Larger k stretches the curve further from the axes.
options: y = 12/x is closer to the origin than y = 4/x
Both curves in 1st and 3rd quadrants. y=12/x is further from the origin than y=4/x.
Both curves appear in all four quadrants
The two curves are identical in shape but reflected

questions.question: Sketch y = 4/x² and y = 10/x² on separate diagrams.
questions.explanation: y=k/x² is always positive for k>0. Larger k gives curves further from origin.
options: y = 4/x² is further from the origin than y = 10/x²
Both curves appear in all four quadrants
Both curves in 1st and 2nd quadrants only (always positive). y=10/x² is further from the origin.
Both curves cross the x-axis at two points

questions.question: Sketch y = x² and y = x(x² - 1) on the same axes and state the number of points of intersection.
questions.explanation: x² = x³ - x => x³ - x² - x = 0 => x(x² - x - 1) = 0. Three real solutions: x=0 and x=(1±√5)/2.
options: 3 points of intersection at x = 0, x = (1+√5)/2, x = (1-√5)/2.

questions.question: Explain why there are no real solutions to 2x² - x + 3 = 0 using the graphs of y = x² + 1 and 2y = x - 1.
questions.explanation: Substituting y = (x-1)/2 into y = x²+1 gives x²-0.5x+1.5=0. Discriminant = 0.25-6 < 0.
options: The line 2y = x - 1 and the curve y = x² + 1 do not intersect, so the equation has no real solutions.
The discriminant equals zero, so there is one repeated root
The line is tangent to the curve at one point
The curves intersect only in the complex plane

question_types.title: Translating graphs 4E — Vertical and Horizontal Shifts

questions.question: Apply f(x + 2) to y = f(x) where f(x) = x². State the coordinates of the axis intercepts.
The graph y = 1/x is translated so that the vertical asymptote is at x = 4. Write the equation of the transformed function.
questions.explanation: Vertical asymptote at x=4 means denominator is zero at x=4: replace x with (x-4).

question_types.title: Stretching graphs 4F — Vertical and Horizontal Stretches

questions.question: Apply f(2x) to y = f(x) where f(x) = x². Describe the transformation.
questions.explanation: Replacing x with 2x gives a horizontal stretch of scale factor 1/2.
options: f(2x) = 2x²; vertical stretch by scale factor 2
f(2x) = 4x²; horizontal stretch by scale factor 2
f(2x) = x²/4; horizontal stretch by scale factor 2
f(2x) = 4x². Horizontal stretch by scale factor 1/2.

questions.question: Apply 0.5f(x) to y = f(x) where f(x) = 1/x. Describe the transformation and write the new equation.
questions.explanation: Multiplying f(x) by 0.5 gives a vertical stretch of scale factor 0.5.

questions.question: The curve y = f(x) has point C(4, 4). Give the coordinates of C after the transformation f(x + 1).
questions.explanation: f(x+1) is a horizontal translation by -1. (4-1, 4) = (3, 4).

questions.question: The curve y = f(x) has point C(4, 4). Give the coordinates of C after the transformation f(x) - 4.
questions.explanation: f(x)-4 is a vertical translation by -4. (4, 4-4) = (4, 0).

questions.question: The curve y = f(x) has horizontal asymptote y = 2 and vertical asymptote x = 1. State the asymptotes after the transformation f(x) + 2.
questions.explanation: f(x)+2 shifts all y-values up by 2. y=2 becomes y=4. Vertical asymptote is unaffected.
options: Vertical asymptote: x = 1 (unchanged); Horizontal asymptote: y = 2
Vertical asymptote: x = 0; Horizontal asymptote: y = 4
Vertical asymptote: x = −1; Horizontal asymptote: y = 4
Vertical asymptote: x = 1 (unchanged); Horizontal asymptote: y = 4.
